claim: MEK inhibitors are not effective in RAS-driven mouse models of cancer.

result.claim: MEK inhibitors are not effective in RAS-driven mouse models of cancer.
result.reasoning:
The claim that MEK inhibitors are not effective in RAS-driven mouse models of cancer is refuted by multiple pieces of evidence.

Paper ID 207681224 ("PIK3CA mutation uncouples tumor growth and Cyclin D1 regulation from MEK/ERK and mutant KRAS signaling") demonstrates that a MEK inhibitor (PD0325901) resulted in "significant anti-cancer activity in a subset of KRAS mutant tumors in vitro and in vivo." This directly contradicts the claim. The paper further explains that while co-occurring PIK3CA mutations can lead to resistance to MEK inhibition alone, tumors with both KRAS and PI3K mutations require combined inhibition of MEK and PI3K/AKT pathways for effective treatment.

Paper ID 4961038 ("Effective Use of PI3K and MEK Inhibitors to Treat Mutant K-Ras G12D and PIK3CA H1047R Murine Lung Cancers") shows that while single-agent NVP-BEZ235 (a PI3K/mTOR inhibitor) did not substantially affect Kras-mutant lung cancers, combining it with a MEK inhibitor (ARRY-142886) resulted in "marked synergy in shrinking these Kras-mutant cancers." This indicates that MEK inhibitors are effective, particularly in combination, in RAS-driven models.

Paper ID 2249004 ("Oncogenic NRAS signaling differentially regulates survival and proliferation in melanoma") reports that pharmacological inhibition of MEK in an inducible mouse model of NRAS-mutant melanoma "activates apoptosis." While it did not induce cell-cycle arrest, the activation of apoptosis signifies a degree of effectiveness. The paper also notes that combining MEK and CDK4 inhibitors led to "substantial synergy in therapeutic efficacy" in these NRAS-mutant melanomas.

In summary, MEK inhibitors have shown anti-cancer activity, induced apoptosis, and demonstrated synergistic effects in combination therapies within various RAS-driven mouse models, directly refuting the claim of their ineffectiveness.

evidence.text: The study used an inducible mouse model of NRAS-mutant melanoma.
evidence.source: Oncogenic NRAS signaling differentially regulates survival and proliferation in melanoma

evidence.text: Bromodomain inhibition leads to a decrease in virulence in a mouse model of infection.
evidence.source: Bromodomain Proteins Contribute to Maintenance of Bloodstream Form Stage Identity in the African Trypanosome

evidence.text: Inhibition of HDAC6 activity using a clinically applicable small-molecule inhibitor significantly improved the survival of mice bearing ARID1A-mutated tumours.
evidence.source: ARID1A-mutated ovarian cancers depend on HDAC6 activity

evidence.text: The ALK kinase inhibitor TAE684 produced greater tumor regression and improved overall survival compared with carboplatin and paclitaxel in the genetically engineered mouse model.
evidence.source: Inhibition of ALK, PI3K/MEK, and HSP90 in Murine Lung Adenocarcinoma Induced by EML4-ALK Fusion Oncogene

evidence.text: NVP-BEZ235 inhibited PI3K signaling in trastuzumab-resistant BT474 H1047R breast cancer xenografts.
evidence.source: NVP-BEZ235, a dual PI3K/mTOR inhibitor, prevents PI3K signaling and inhibits the growth of cancer cells with activating PI3K mutations.

evidence.text: LSD1 and PARP1 inhibitors are prosurvival molecular tools.
evidence.source: PARP1‐LSD1 functional interplay controls transcription of SOD2 that protects human pro‐inflammatory macrophages from death under an oxidative condition

evidence.text: mTOR signalling in cancer cells regulates G-CSF.

evidence.text: Tumors with both KRAS and PI3K mutations are unlikely to respond to inhibition of the MEK pathway alone.
evidence.source: PIK3CA mutation uncouples tumor growth and Cyclin D1 regulation from MEK/ERK and mutant KRAS signaling

evidence.text: Ezh2(Y641F) is the most common somatic Ezh2 gain-of-function mutation in mice.
evidence.source: An oncogenic Ezh2 mutation induces tumors through global redistribution of histone 3 lysine 27 trimethylation

evidence.text: Loss of either p53 or Lkb1 impaired the response of Kras-mutant cancers to docetaxel monotherapy in mice.
evidence.source: A murine lung cancer co-clinical trial identifies genetic modifiers of therapeutic response

evidence.text: Direct pharmacological inhibition of the RAS proto-oncogene has thus far been unsuccessful.

evidence.text: Physcion inhibited 6PGD, cancer cell proliferation and tumor growth.

evidence.text: Knockdown of Beclin 1 decreased LH-stimulated StAR expression in TM3 mouse Leydig cells.

evidence.text: NVP-BEZ235 is a dual inhibitor of the PI3K and the mammalian target of rapamycin (mTOR).

evidence.text: NVP-BEZ235 inhibited the proliferation of HER2-amplified BT474 cells exogenously expressing mutations that render them resistant to trastuzumab.
